objective: Design and implement a reliable and scalable pipeline to load MySQL tables from the agentichub database into Google BigQuery (verizon_data_dea dataset), supporting a one-time full historical load for all identified tables followed by ongoing scheduled incremental loads (hourly or daily per table) to underpin analytics, reporting, and long-term data storage for the 5gcore programme.
business_context: The 5gcore programme requires consolidated analytics, reporting, and long-term data storage backed by MySQL source data in the agentichub database. Stakeholders (led by Yash) require independent visibility into data quality, load success, and audit logs without engineering involvement. The pipeline covers three workstreams — historical load, scheduled incremental load, and end-to-end monitoring with audit logging — and is currently in draft status pending stakeholder review.
functional_requirements: Perform a one-time full historical load of all identified MySQL (agentichub) tables into BigQuery dataset verizon_data_dea with 100% data completeness before any incremental loads are activated
Implement scheduled incremental loads running hourly or daily per table using timestamp or primary key-based change tracking to detect new and updated records
Execute all load logic via stored procedure sp_mysqltobq_load.sql with merge key on employee_id and updated_date
Block the incremental load pipeline from starting until full historical load validation is confirmed and passes
Enforce a 60-day data retention policy on the target BigQuery table
Perform schema validation before each load with mismatch logging and a documented strategy for handling new MySQL columns
Perform row count reconciliation between MySQL and BigQuery after every load
Confirm primary key uniqueness post-load and verify null and datatype constraints against schema definitions
Maintain a complete audit log for every load execution capturing status, record counts, and timestamps
Provide stakeholder-accessible dashboards or log views enabling independent review of load success and execution history without engineering involvement
Send alerts promptly on any pipeline failure or data quality violation
Support an initial data volume of 5 GB with ongoing growth of 1 GB/month

source_systems: MySQL — agentichub database via GCP Cloud SQL instance verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore
data_requirements.data_types: Structured relational tabular data from MySQL source schema
data_requirements.volume: 5 GB initial load; 1 GB/month ongoing growth; 60-day retention policy enforced on target BigQuery table
data_requirements.frequency: One-time full historical load followed by scheduled incremental loads run hourly or daily per table

non_functional.performance: Row counts must match between MySQL and BigQuery for every load; no data duplication or data loss permitted in any incremental update
non_functional.scalability: Pipeline must accommodate initial 5 GB load and sustain 1 GB/month data growth
non_functional.latency: Hourly or daily scheduled incremental loads per table; not real-time

security.access_controls: Stakeholder dashboards and audit logs must be independently accessible without engineering involvement

constraints.timeline: Status is draft — pending stakeholder review before implementation proceeds
constraints.technical_limitations: Incremental loads must not begin until full historical load validation passes; new column handling strategy must be documented before implementation

assumptions: Pipeline status is draft and pending stakeholder review before implementation proceeds
All load logic will be executed via stored procedure sp_mysqltobq_load.sql with merge key on employee_id and updated_date
Target BigQuery project is verizon-data, target dataset is verizon_data_dea, and target table is employees

Scheduled incremental loads run without failures on the defined hourly or daily cadence per table
No data duplication or data loss occurs in any incremental update

Audit logs are complete and accessible after each load execution capturing status, record counts, and timestamps

reporting, and long-term data storage for the 5gcore programme. source: verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore (ip: 34.70.79.163, port: 3306, db: agentichub, table: employees, user: sa). Objective: Design and implement a reliable and scalable pipeline to load MySQL tables from the agentichub database into Google BigQuery (verizon_data_dea dataset), supporting a one-time full historical load for all identified tables followed by ongoing scheduled incremental loads (hourly or daily per table) to underpin analytics, reporting, and long-term data storage for the 5gcore programme.

Business Context:
The 5gcore programme requires consolidated analytics, reporting, and long-term data storage backed by MySQL source data in the agentichub database. Stakeholders (led by Yash) require independent visibility into data quality, load success, and audit logs without engineering involvement. The pipeline covers three workstreams — historical load, scheduled incremental load, and end-to-end monitoring with audit logging — and is currently in draft status pending stakeholder review.

Functional Requirements:
Perform a one-time full historical load of all identified MySQL (agentichub) tables into BigQuery dataset verizon_data_dea with 100% data completeness before any incremental loads are activated
Implement scheduled incremental loads running hourly or daily per table using timestamp or primary key-based change tracking to detect new and updated records
Execute all load logic via stored procedure sp_mysqltobq_load.sql with merge key on employee_id and updated_date
Block the incremental load pipeline from starting until full historical load validation is confirmed and passes
Enforce a 60-day data retention policy on the target BigQuery table
Perform schema validation before each load with mismatch logging and a documented strategy for handling new MySQL columns
Perform row count reconciliation between MySQL and BigQuery after every load
Confirm primary key uniqueness post-load and verify null and datatype constraints against schema definitions
Maintain a complete audit log for every load execution capturing status, record counts, and timestamps
Provide stakeholder-accessible dashboards or log views enabling independent review of load success and execution history without engineering involvement
Send alerts promptly on any pipeline failure or data quality violation
Support an initial data volume of 5 GB with ongoing growth of 1 GB/month

Acceptance Criteria:
All agreed-upon MySQL tables are present in BigQuery with verified row counts matching the source
Historical data is 100% complete in BigQuery before any incremental loads are activated
Scheduled incremental loads run without failures on the defined hourly or daily cadence per table
No data duplication or data loss occurs in any incremental update
Change detection correctly identifies and loads only new or updated records using timestamp or primary key tracking
Merge key on employee_id and updated_date is applied via sp_mysqltobq_load.sql for all incremental loads
60-day retention policy is enforced on the target BigQuery table
Schema compatibility is validated before each load with mismatch events logged
New column handling strategy is documented and consistently applied across all pipeline loads
Primary key uniqueness is confirmed post-load for every execution
Null and datatype constraints are verified against schema definitions after every load
Row counts match between MySQL and BigQuery for both historical and incremental loads
Audit logs are complete and accessible after each load execution capturing status, record counts, and timestamps
Stakeholder dashboards display current load status and full execution history
Alerts fire promptly on any pipeline failure or data quality violation

  • mysql / agentichub | instance: verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore | 34.70.79.163:3306 | user: sa | tables: employees
